name: Knowledge Capture & Debugging
description: 3 agents build a data pipeline, hit bugs, capture lessons, then a new agent onboards using search. Exercises: lessons, FTS5 search, file actions, graph analytics.

cycles.title: Pipeline design with file artifacts
cycles.scene: Maya designs the pipeline architecture and writes the spec to disk.

actions.content:
Data Pipeline Architecture
=========================
Sources: 3 Kafka topics (events, metrics, logs)
Schema: Avro with schema registry (Confluent)
Throughput target: 50k events/sec sustained
Latency SLA: < 5s end-to-end

Flow:
  Kafka -> Ingestion (validate + DLQ) -> Transform (clean + enrich) -> Storage (TimescaleDB)

Partitioning: by tenant_id, 7-day chunks
Retention: 90d raw, 2yr rollup via continuous aggregates

actions.body: Pipeline design written to docs/pipeline-design.txt. Key decision: TimescaleDB over plain PostgreSQL for time-series. Partitioning by tenant_id.

cycles.title: Parallel build — ingestion and transform
cycles.scene: Maya builds ingestion while Noah starts transform. Both write code artifacts to disk.

actions.content:
Kafka Consumer Config
====================
group.id: pipeline-ingestion-v1
auto.offset.reset: earliest
enable.auto.commit: false
max.poll.records: 500

Deserialization: Avro with schema registry
DLQ topic: pipeline.dead-letter
DLQ routing: malformed schema OR missing required fields

Transform Rules
===============
1. Normalize timestamps to UTC (reject future timestamps > 5min)
2. Trim whitespace from string fields
3. Enrich with tenant metadata from reference table
4. Drop records with null tenant_id (log to metrics)
5. Deduplicate by (event_id, tenant_id) within 1h window

cycles.title: Ingestion bug — Kafka offset commit failure
cycles.scene: Maya hits a nasty Kafka bug: offsets aren't committing properly, causing duplicate processing.

actions.body: BUG: Kafka consumer offsets not committing. After rebalance, consumer re-processes ~2000 events. Suspect auto.commit is interfering with manual commit. Need to debug.

actions.reason: Kafka offset commit failure causing duplicate processing

actions.content:
Debug Session: Kafka Offset Commit
===================================
Symptom: After consumer group rebalance, ~2000 events reprocessed
Attempt 1: Set enable.auto.commit=false — still happening
Attempt 2: Added commitSync() after each batch — still happening
Root cause: Consumer was calling commitAsync() BEFORE processing
  completed. On rebalance, in-flight batch offsets were lost.
Fix: Move commitSync() to AFTER successful batch processing.
  Also added idempotency check (event_id dedup in Redis) as safety net.

actions.body: Good debugging. Commit the fix and capture this as a lesson — it'll bite other teams too.

actions.command: lesson add "Kafka offset commit ordering" --problem "Consumer offsets lost on rebalance causing duplicate processing — commitAsync called before batch processing completed" --solution "Move commitSync to after successful batch processing. Add idempotency layer (event_id dedup in Redis) as safety net" --learned "Always commit offsets AFTER processing, never before. Async commit is fine for performance but add idempotency for correctness" --pattern "kafka-consumer"

cycles.title: Transform bug — timezone normalization edge case
cycles.scene: Noah discovers timestamps from one source are in a non-standard format, breaking the transform.

actions.body: Found edge case: source-3 sends timestamps as Unix millis (not ISO 8601 like sources 1-2). Transform rule #1 was parsing all as ISO and silently dropping source-3 records.

actions.content:
Debug: Timezone Normalization
=============================
Source 1: ISO 8601 with timezone (2026-03-06T10:30:00Z)
Source 2: ISO 8601 with timezone (2026-03-06T10:30:00+05:30)
Source 3: Unix milliseconds (1741257000000) <-- DIFFERENT!

Impact: ~30% of records silently dropped (all source-3)
Fix: Detect format by checking if value is numeric.
  If numeric and > 1e12, treat as Unix millis.
  If numeric and > 1e9, treat as Unix seconds.
  Otherwise parse as ISO 8601.

actions.command: lesson add "Multi-format timestamp normalization" --problem "Transform silently dropped 30% of records because source-3 used Unix millis while others used ISO 8601" --solution "Auto-detect timestamp format: numeric > 1e12 = millis, numeric > 1e9 = seconds, else ISO 8601. Log format detection results to metrics" --learned "Never assume uniform timestamp formats across data sources. Always validate record counts per source to catch silent data loss early" --pattern "data-pipeline"

cycles.title: New agent onboards — uses search and graph to ramp up
cycles.scene: Olivia joins the team mid-project. She uses search, lessons, graph analytics, and file reading to understand the system.

actions.body: Ramped up via search + lessons. I see build-storage is ready. I'll take it — TimescaleDB hypertables with tenant_id partitioning, right?

cycles.title: Storage implementation with knowledge reuse
cycles.scene: Olivia builds storage, referencing lessons from earlier bugs to avoid similar pitfalls.

actions.content:
TimescaleDB Schema
==================
CREATE TABLE events (
  event_id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
  tenant_id UUID NOT NULL,
  event_type TEXT NOT NULL,
  payload JSONB NOT NULL,
  source TEXT NOT NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
  ingested_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

SELECT create_hypertable('events', 'created_at',
  chunk_time_interval => INTERVAL '7 days',
  partitioning_column => 'tenant_id',
  number_partitions => 4
);

-- Idempotency: UPSERT by event_id (learned from Kafka offset lesson)
INSERT INTO events ... ON CONFLICT (event_id) DO NOTHING;

-- Continuous aggregate for rollups
CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW events_hourly
WITH (timescaledb.continuous) AS
SELECT tenant_id, event_type,
  time_bucket('1 hour', created_at) AS bucket,
  COUNT(*) AS event_count
FROM events
GROUP BY tenant_id, event_type, bucket;

-- Retention: 90d raw, 2yr rollup
SELECT add_retention_policy('events', INTERVAL '90 days');
SELECT add_retention_policy('events_hourly', INTERVAL '730 days');

actions.body: Storage schema written to src/storage/schema.txt. Used UPSERT for idempotency (learned from Maya's Kafka lesson). Hypertables with 7-day chunks, 4 tenant partitions.

cycles.title: Integration validation and final lessons
cycles.scene: The team validates the full pipeline and captures final lessons.

actions.content:
Integration Validation Results
==============================
Test 1: Produce 10k events across 3 sources -> OK (10k stored)
Test 2: Malformed Avro record -> routed to DLQ -> OK
Test 3: Source-3 Unix millis timestamps -> normalized correctly -> OK
Test 4: Duplicate event_id -> deduplicated via UPSERT -> OK
Test 5: Consumer rebalance mid-batch -> no duplicates (idempotency) -> OK
Test 6: Continuous aggregate -> hourly rollup correct -> OK
Test 7: Retention policy -> records >90d marked for drop -> OK

All 7 tests PASSED. Pipeline ready for production.

actions.command: lesson add "Onboarding via knowledge graph" --problem "New team member joining mid-project needs to understand architecture, past decisions, and known pitfalls quickly" --solution "Use fl search to find relevant entities, fl lesson list/show to learn from past bugs, read file artifacts for design docs, fl pagerank to find high-importance entities" --learned "Structured knowledge capture during development pays off immediately when new people onboard. Lessons with patterns enable cross-project learning" --pattern "team-onboarding"
